caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is 4-digit 5414 and is contaminated by sibling interior and graphic-design occupations.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fashion is a hypothesis for the dominant 541490 revenue subsegment, not a verified code-level revenue split.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: No source maps AI task exposure directly to the supplied wage shares.
inputs.pi_dist: Marketplace work is a pricing and supply proxy, not a census of U.S. design-studio contracts.
inputs.pi_moat: The assessment is for the hypothesized fashion-design dominant subsegment and does not establish a code-wide moat.
inputs.t50_years: Neither source reports a U.S. 541490 establishment-level adoption rate.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The direct 51.7% observation is not code-specific and includes arts/design alumni outside specialized design services.
inputs.historical_analogs: No 541490 historical price or margin series was located.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: The source covers U.S. firms with two to 99 employees and up to $25M revenue, not 541490 or owners aged 60+ specifically.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Do not turn generic owner retirement pressure into a target-code roll-up supply claim.
inputs.active_consolidators: Absence of confirmed public evidence is not proof that no private buyer exists.
inputs.buy_mult: No observed 541490 lower-middle-market transaction multiple was found.
inputs.exit_mult: This is a stress assumption, not a direct transaction observation; broad fashion brand M&A is intentionally excluded as a comp.
inputs.pricing_structure: Marketplace prices and billing modes may not represent every U.S. specialized design studio.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $308,134 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541490); class <5: 2,871 firms, avg revenue/firm $385,018 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $60,255 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 302 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,501,623 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $235,004 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 131 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,384,664 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $529,700 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 57 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,240,281 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,915,604 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $43,541,688 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,814,274 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 9 firms, avg revenue/firm $30,497,444 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,772,850 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 82

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: The model preserves the supplied 4-digit wage shares but treats the unlisted residual as the likely mix of specialized design, production coordination and some physical sample/fabrication work. Visual ideation, CAD and technical documentation are exposed; physical fitting, materials and production handoff curb total replaceability.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541490 ($2,027,827,545) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541490 ($3,671,109,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact artifact bindings pass. The reopened sources support the stated fashion-workflow, adoption, pricing, succession, and M&A-boundary facts; the record remains useful because every material code-scope, role-mix, marketplace, adoption, and transaction-evidence limitation is explicitly disclosed and the resulting kill thesis does not depend on hiding those proxies.

sources.title: SNAAP, Adoption, Applications, and Attitudes Toward AI Among Arts and Design Alumni
sources.what_it_supports: U.S. Fall-2025 survey of more than 2,000 arts and design alumni: 51.7% reported professional generative-AI use, primarily as support for productivity, idea development and editing. Used as an adoption proxy, not a 541490 census.

sources.title: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, Fashion Designers Occupational Outlook Handbook
sources.what_it_supports: Fashion-designer duties include CAD, trend research, fabric selection, prototypes, presentations and final-production oversight; fashion designers work in design firms and across apparel/manufacturing contexts. Used to distinguish digital from physical work.

sources.title: McKinsey and The Business of Fashion, State of Fashion 2026
sources.what_it_supports: More than 35% of fashion and luxury executives report using generative AI in selected functions including image creation and product discovery; fashion companies are applying automation to routine work. It describes brands and fashion companies, not independent 541490 studios.

sources.title: Upwork, Best Freelance Fashion Designers for Hire
sources.what_it_supports: Fashion-design marketplace guide describing ideation, tech packs, sourcing, production coordination, hourly or milestone payment, and project budgets for garments, tech packs, collections and launches. Used for pricing structure and global freelance competition.

sources.title: U.S. Bank, 2025 Small Business Perspective Survey
sources.what_it_supports: National survey of 1,000 U.S. businesses with two to 99 employees and up to $25M revenue: more than half of owners are over 55 and only 54% report a succession plan. Used only as a general size-band owner-age proxy.

sources.title: Vogue, The State of Fashion M&A in 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Fashion deal coverage centers on brand, retail, licensing and portfolio transactions, with conservative valuations amid macro pressure. Used to explain why fashion-brand M&A cannot be treated as evidence of a 541490 design-services roll-up market.

title: Other Specialized Design Services
